items.prompt: exposure: scripts/.discord_webhook.txt in Scheduler is git-tracked and contains a live discord.com/api/webhooks URL (first committed in cf05134, feat(scheduler): v0.2.0). Mitigate: (1) add `scripts/.discord_webhook.txt` to .gitignore; (2) `git rm --cached scripts/.discord_webhook.txt`; (3) commit the redaction; (4) rotate the webhook on Discord (the leaked URL is permanently in git history and trivially recoverable). History scrub via `git filter-repo --path scripts/.discord_webhook.txt --invert-paths` is a separate manual call after rotation. Compare YaC + YaApothecary, which already use `.discord_webhook.txt.example` + gitignored real file.
items.notes: Surfaced by working-tree-scan-weekly 2026-05-29. Same secret-file pattern as the YaB .finances/ exposure earlier in the cycle.
items.note: Repo-side mitigation verified done 2026-06-10: scripts/.discord_webhook.txt no longer tracked at HEAD and .gitignore covers it (line 53). Residual: the URL lives in git history (cf05134), so the webhook itself needs rotation in Discord (user action), then paste the new URL into scripts/.discord_webhook.txt. History scrub optional if repo stays private.

items.prompt: YaC has one stash entry: `stash@{0}: On main: scraper WIP`. Review and either apply (then ship or re-stash with clearer label), or drop. `git -C X:\YesAndChains stash show -p stash@{0}` to inspect; `git stash apply stash@{0}` or `git stash drop stash@{0}` to act. Stashes live in the reflog and can be reconstructed for 90 days, so the safer move is `apply` and decide in-tree, not blind drop.

items.title: After 0.34.2 ships: repoint live tool references to tools/_archived/ (CONTEXT.md:210 scrape_pipeline.py; BACKLOG.md:395/496/1051 seed_courses.py + scrape_pipeline.py). Leave historical changelog rows untouched.

Do NOT rewrite historical CONTEXT.md changelog entries that name these tools. | 2026-06-04 17:08Z drain: blocking dep yac-mnew-ship-0.34.2 now COMPLETED (0.34.2 shipped, archive move committed), so unblocked. CONTEXT.md:235 names scrape_pipeline.py inside a LIVE scraper-workflow description while the tool now lives in tools/_archived/ and is absent from the CLAUDE.md 'six active scripts' list -- that's a live-vs-dead-workflow judgment, not a repoint. BACKLOG.md 248/398/499 + CONTEXT.md:9 are historical/forward-fix rows -> leave untouched per item spec. 218KB CONTEXT.md = FUSE risk; needs a focused session. Left pending. | 2026-06-05 13:10Z drain: grounded the remaining scope. CONTEXT.md refs are all historical EXCEPT line ~236 (the 'Course Data Gathering -- Sonnet' manual scraper-session description, which names scrape_pipeline.py as the live tool). That workflow was superseded by ADR 0021 (local cron via cron_course_refresh.py + run-course-refresh.ps1 / Windows Task Scheduler); CLAUDE.md's six-active-scripts list does not mention scrape_pipeline.py at all. BACKLOG 30.2 ('Update tools/scrape_pipeline.py to write to per-state shard') is likely obsolete for the same reason. So this is NOT a path repoint -- it is a confirm-and-retire of a dead manual workflow in a 336KB CONTEXT.md over FUSE. Recommend: Nick confirms the manual scraper session is retired, then rewrite CONTEXT.md:236 to describe the cron pipeline and mark BACKLOG 30.2 done/obsolete in a focused session. Left pending.
items.note: Drain 2026-06-07: original line numbers (CONTEXT.md:210, BACKLOG.md:395/496/1051) are stale (added pre-0.34.2, repo now 0.50.0). Re-survey done. CLAUDE.md + ADR 0021 indicate ingestion moved to cron_course_refresh.py -> course_parser.py via Windows Task Scheduler. NEEDS NICK: confirm the manual scraper session is retired, then rewrite CONTEXT.md 252-258 to the cron model. Not a mechanical repoint; left for attended review.

items.title: Portfolio-wide unkillable .git/index.lock blocks every git write
items.prompt: All six repos carry a stale .git/index.lock that cannot be removed from the agent sandbox (rm returns 'Operation not permitted' across the FUSE permission boundary). Timestamps differ per repo (YaB/YaA 12:22, Scheduler 13:08, BR 18:31, HBH/YaC 18:57) so it is not one process - it is the recurring FUSE lock-survival hazard now hitting the whole portfolio at once. EFFECT: no commit/add/push/release works from any session; the audit-to-commit loop, BR's staged v0.57.x release, and the YaB doc-batch drain are all wedged behind it. FIX (Windows side, Nick): run the git-unstick skill or unstick-git.ps1 against each repo (it force-removes the lock from the Windows side), then the queued doc-commit + release items become drainable again. Until then every commit-bearing queue item is structurally blocked.
items.blockedReason: stale .git/index.lock unremovable from sandbox on all 6 repos; needs Windows-side git-unstick / unstick-git.ps1
items.notes: Surfaced by loop-tick-frequent 2026-06-05T21:10. Generalizes the YaB-only block (yab-doc-batch-blocked-lock-and-voice-2026-06-05) to the full portfolio. Supersedes per-repo lock diagnosis - clear all six in one git-unstick pass.

items.title: YaB stalled-tree close-out: clean .bak, set EOL policy, fix BOM, ship in two commits
items.prompt: YaB's 2026-06-06 bar-raise verdict is 'stalled' (fifth consecutive). Head is e6cac08 (2026-05-29), eight days cold, and the working tree is now 26+ uncommitted items that are no longer doc-only, so the old 'one doc-only commit closes it' plan is dead. Close it out in this order: (1) git-unstick the unremovable .git/index.lock from the Windows side first (sandbox rm returns Operation-not-permitted); (2) delete scripts/discord-notify.ps1.bak and scripts/uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1.bak, or add '*.bak' to .gitignore; (3) fix the UTF-8 BOM (ef bb bf) the overnight drift pass stamped onto scripts/discord-notify.ps1 and scripts/uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1 (release.ps1 does not carry a BOM); (4) add a .gitattributes with '* text=auto' and renormalize once so the CRLF flip stops recurring every session; (5) fix em-dash voice violations in docs/DECISIONS.md (D-001..D-007) and the CANONICAL_AUDIT reports before commit (run solo-dev-voice-audit), and decide whether the auto-generated audit reports get the same scrub or are exempted; (6) ship in two coherent commits through scripts/release.ps1: 'docs(yab): land F1-F11 canonical batch + 05-29..06-06 audit/bar-raise reports' and 'chore(yab): cross-project cwd guard (CLAUDE.md command rule + script self-location)'.
items.blockedReason: unremovable .git/index.lock (needs Windows-side git-unstick) + em-dash voice fixes; not auto-drainable from the sandbox
items.notes: Surfaced by loop-tick-frequent 2026-06-06T21:53 from BAR_RAISE-2026-06-06.md Maintainability HIGH (carried 9 cycles). Supersedes the doc-only framing of yab-doc-batch-blocked-lock-and-voice-2026-06-05: the tree now mixes code + .bak + BOM + EOL policy, so it is a 2-commit structural job, not a bounded doc tick. Related: wts-2026-06-05-yab-stale-working-tree (P2), wts-2026-06-05-portfolio-wide-index-lock (P0).

items.title: YaB commit unblocked: stale .git/index.lock cleared — ship the now-committable tree
items.prompt: The 2026-06-07 canonical audit found and removed the zero-byte stale .git/index.lock (dated 2026-06-05 07:22, no git process) that was the real root cause of YaB's 8+ cycles of 'nothing commits' — git status worked but every git add/commit failed. The Windows-side Remove-Item succeeded where sandbox FUSE rm could not; write path verified (add/reset take and release the lock cleanly). Step 1 of yab-barraise-closeout-2026-06-06 (Windows-side git-unstick) is now DONE. Proceed with the rest from any environment: (1) add .gitattributes '* text=auto' (+ '*.ps1 text eol=crlf' if scripts want CRLF) and git add --renormalize . once, BEFORE committing, so the CRLF flip stops recurring; (2) delete scripts/discord-notify.ps1.bak and scripts/uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1.bak (or add '*.bak' to .gitignore); (3) fix the UTF-8 BOM on discord-notify.ps1 and uninstall-yab-protocol.ps1 to match release.ps1; (4) the tree now ALSO carries a v0.11.0 frontend polish pass (6 components + 4 hooks + 6 modified web files) — see yab-polish-log-v0-11-0-release-claim and yab-polish-orphan-files before committing; (5) ship in coherent units via scripts/release.ps1: feat(yab): v0.11.0 polish pass / docs(yab): canonical batch + 05-29..06-07 reports / chore(yab): cross-project cwd guard. Committing is Nick's call per repo convention.

items.title: Reconcile POLISH_LOG.md v0.11.0 'shipped' claim with reality (nothing is at 0.11.0)
items.prompt: docs/POLISH_LOG.md line 3 says '...shipped together. All items applied; release at v0.11.0' and the Release section (line 148) declares v0.11.0 — but no version marker agrees: all four package.json read 0.10.0, the App.tsx pill (line 117) is v0.10.0, CHANGELOG.md has no 0.11.0 entry, DESIGN.md does not mention v0.11.0, and HEAD is e6cac08. The polish CONTENT is genuinely applied (format.ts has all 7 claimed helpers; index.css has the claimed utilities; the components exist) — only the RELEASE is fictional. Resolve one of two ways: EITHER actually ship v0.11.0 via scripts/release.ps1 (bump all 4 package.json + the App.tsx pill + DESIGN.md header 'Version:' + DESIGN §10 milestone row + a CHANGELOG v0.11.0 entry), OR downgrade POLISH_LOG.md language from 'shipped/release at v0.11.0' to 'applied to working tree, pending v0.11.0 release' so an in-repo doc stops asserting a release state that doesn't exist. Tie to yab-index-lock-cleared-commit-now (the release is now unblocked).

By contrast Tooltip (4 refs), Badge, Spinner (via ui/index.ts barrel) and useEscapeKey ARE wired. Before the v0.11.0 commit, either wire each orphan into the page/component it was authored for, or delete it, so dead code does not ship under a 'cohesive polish cohort' banner. Decision needed per file (wire vs drop), so not auto-safe.

items.title: YaE git index file corrupt - blocks all commits/release.ps1
items.prompt: git status on X:\YesAndEverything returns: error: index uses 4M?\x9c extension, which we do not understand / fatal: index file corrupt. git log still reads (object store intact) so this is index-file corruption, NOT just a stale .git/index.lock. FIX (Windows side, Nick): run git-unstick / unstick-git.ps1, which for a corrupt index should back up .git/index then remove it and run git reset to rebuild from HEAD (lock removal alone will not clear corruption). EFFECT: no commit, push, or release.ps1 works against YaE until rebuilt; the card-pill sweep (yae-card-pills-sweep-2026-06-07) is blocked behind this. Related to portfolio-wide lock item wts-2026-06-05-portfolio-wide-index-lock but a distinct, worse symptom (corruption vs lock).
items.blockedReason: corrupt .git/index unrepairable from sandbox (rm Operation not permitted across FUSE); needs Windows-side git reset rebuild

Bump only the MINOR digit on each (vX.Y.x form). NOT auto_safe: index.html is FUSE-truncation-sensitive, edit via Python atomic-write-with-readback and ship through release.ps1. Other three pills (Brackish v0.57.x, Scheduler v0.3.0, Budget v0.10.x) are already current.

Convert scripts/write-dashboard-status.ps1 to read-modify-write: parse existing status/data/YaC.json (or X:\YesAndEverything\status\data\YaC.json), preserve the entire barRaise block and audit.findings, mutate only release-owned fields (version, lastReleaseAt, lastReleaseMessage, workTreeClean), parse real high/medium/low counts from the latest CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md, then atomic-write. Land BEFORE 0.48.0 ships (staged release will undo the self-healed JSON). NOTE: currently lock-blocked Windows-side (index.lock EPERM) - apply once the portfolio-wide lock clears.
items.notes: Enqueued by self-reprompt-loop 2026-06-07 rule-3 (latest portfolio bar-raise YaC 06-07 at-risk). Was absent from queue across 12 bar-raise cycles. Mirror gap exists for HBH write-dashboard-status.ps1 (see breadcrumb). | 2026-06-10 21:10Z drain (verification, no code touched): RMW conversion is ALREADY APPLIED in the YaC working tree (scripts/write-dashboard-status.ps1, +15/-1 uncommitted). Deviation: findings counts are preserved from prior JSON, not parsed fresh from latest CANONICAL_AUDIT-*.md; acceptable since canonical-audit owns those counts. REMAINING: commit only. Blocked by YaC .git/index.lock (zero-byte, 2026-06-08 21:02, unremovable from sandbox). Once Nick clears locks Windows-side, this lands as one commit; no further code work needed.

items.prompt: PROJECT_SPEC.md §2 Non-goals lists 'Image-based labels (the look is all SVG and webfonts, on purpose).' That claim is contradicted by the shipped renderer: labels now use PNG symbols (data/symbols/), PNG botanical illustrations (data/illustrations/), and JPG parchment textures (data/textures/). Reframe the non-goal as 'no per-label user-uploaded raster art' and acknowledge the curated PNG/JPG asset libraries as core.

PROJECT_SPEC.md §4 has no schema for data/rune-meanings.json, which is loaded at src/main.js:202. It is a rune-char to meaning map (e.g. {"ᚠ": "Wealth & Fortune"}). §4.3 documents runes.json as [{c,n}] and §4.1 puts meaning inline on the herb record as m. Add a §4.x schema documenting rune-meanings.json shape and how it resolves relative to runes.json and the herb-record runes array.

X:\YesAndApothecary\CLAUDE.md data-table drift. The data/symbols.js row calls it the 'Celtic symbol SVG registry'; it is PNG-only now (locked decision v0.8.2). The table also omits three live items: data/rune-meanings.json, the data/symbols/ PNG directory (+ SOURCES.md), and the data/textures/ JPG directory (+ SOURCES.md). Fix the symbols.js description and add the three rows. CLAUDE.md is a handler file: surface for Nick, do not auto-edit.

Locked-decision violation in shipped YaB code: apps/api/src/server.ts:758 returns a generic 400 where decision D-011 mandates a 409. Add one branch before the generic 400, mirroring the existing BackupFailedError branch: else if ((e as any)?.code === "account_plaid_driven") return c.json({ error: (e as Error).message, code: "account_plaid_driven" }, 409); Then run cd X:\YesAndBudget; .\scripts\release.ps1 -Message "fix(yab): D-011 409 on account_plaid_driven" -Bump patch. Verify with the existing api tests before release.
